Title: Deletion of the gene encoding the magnesium chelatase I subunit resulted in a novel wheat leaf colour mutant

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a second recessive locus required for the yellow-leaf phenotype in combination with Y1-7A
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic segregation analysis; BSA-Seq peak detection on chromosome 7D
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential mapping target for future cloning of chlorophyll-deficiency loci; currently not directly usable because causal gene is unresolved
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mapped broadly to chromosome 7D (94.35–619.63 Mb by BSA-Seq). The paper notes cloning difficulty due to probable centromeric proximity and possible rye introgression/structural variation. No causal gene was cloned or functionally validated.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Genetic analysis showed the yellow-leaf phenotype is controlled by two recessive loci. 2. BSA-Seq identified Y2-7D as a second locus on chromosome 7D. 3. The causal gene for Y2-7D remains unresolved in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Chinese Spring reference / SN388-2 mapping population
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Putative DNA-dependent ATPase superfamily; CHLI ATPase with conserved Walker motifs
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed step / early branch point toward chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as the I subunit of Mg-chelatase to drive insertion of Mg2+ into protoporphyrin IX, forming Mg-protoporphyrin IX
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms immediate precursor and commits flux toward chlorophyll production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion of Y1-7A reduces chlorophyll accumulation, increases the Proto IX/Mg-Proto IX ratio, impairs chloroplast development, and causes yellow leaves at the seedling stage
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal field conditions versus transfer to an artificial climate chamber during greening/revival
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a crucial/bottleneck step for Mg-Proto IX biosynthesis and chlorophyll formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BSA-Seq; fine mapping; whole-genome resequencing; IGV read-depth inspection; PCR deletion markers; Sanger sequencing; qRT-PCR; transient GFP localization; metabolite profiling of Proto IX and Mg-Proto IX; pigment quantification; TEM
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in green tissues, especially leaves; weaker in seeds and roots
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Seedling-stage yellow leaves and impaired chloroplast ultrastructure under field conditions; chloroplast fragmentation accompanies chlorophyll deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker gene for chlorophyll-related breeding and molecular diagnosis; avoid knockout/deletion because of chlorophyll deficiency, but allele mining/editing could tune leaf colour and photosynthetic traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The causal lesion is a 5311 bp deletion encompassing the entire Y1-7A sequence in SN388-2. The yellow phenotype is genetically controlled by two recessive loci (Y1-7A and Y2-7D), but this paper experimentally validates Y1-7A only. Yellow leaves revert to green in the revival stage or under artificial climate chamber conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Y1-7A was fine-mapped to a 0.3 Mb interval on chromosome 7AL and identified as TraesCS7A03G1163900. 2. SN388-2 carries a 5.3 kb deletion spanning the entire gene, confirmed by resequencing, PCR and Sanger sequencing. 3. Loss of Y1-7A blocks the Mg-chelatase step from Proto IX to Mg-Proto IX, elevates the Proto IX/Mg-Proto IX ratio, reduces chlorophyll, and disrupts chloroplast development, causing yellow leaves.
